content.problemStatement:
You are given a convex polygon PPP with vertices P0,P1,…,Pn−1P_0, P_1, \ldots, P_{n-1}P0​,P1​,…,Pn−1​, each having integer coordinates. On each edge PiP(i+1)%nP_{i} P_{(i+1) \% n}Pi​P(i+1)%n​ of the polygon, choose a point RiR_iRi​ uniformly at random. What is the expected area of the convex hull of these nnn chosen points R0,R1,…Rn−1R_0, R_1, \ldots R_{n-1}R0​,R1​,…Rn−1​?

**Note:**


Consider the area of the convex hull as zero if it contains less than 3 vertices.
All the points RiR_iRi​ are chosen independently of each other.
Your answer is considered correct if and only if its absolute or relative error doesn't exceed 10−610^{-6}10−6.

Input Format

The first line contains nnn, the number of vertices in the convex polygon.
The next nnn lines contain the coordinates of the vertices of the polygon in anti-clockwise order.


Output Format
For each testcase, print the expected area of the convex hull of the nnn randomly chosen points.
constraints: The absolute values of all the coordinates ≤107\leq 10^7≤107.
All the points in the input are distinct.
The described polygon PPP is convex and the vertices of the polygon are given in anti-clockwise order. Also, no three vertices of the polygon are collinear.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The expected area of the convex hull of the randomly chosen points is calculated to be 0.12500000000.12500000000.1250000000.
